PASS (with one critical security caveat): the rewritten `backend/scripts/seed_demo.py` substantially achieves the task objective of seeding all audit modules with deterministic, idempotent demo data, and the `seed_document_versions` no-op is acceptable given the FK mismatch.

Critical findings / analysis:
- Functional coverage is broad and aligned to the objective: universe, plans/items, engagements/team/time, workpapers/procedures/sampling, findings/recommendations/responses, issues/action plans/follow-ups/escalations, surveys/responses, documents, charter/clauses, CCM/anomalies/exceptions, QAIP, assurance gaps, test scripts, skills/capacity, committee stack, reporting/board packs, and external auditor sessions are all seeded.
- Determinism is correctly implemented: all seeded dates are derived from fixed `BASE = datetime(2026,1,1)` via `d(...)`; no runtime `now()` is used in seed keys.
- Idempotency design is sound: `upsert()` performs key-based existence checks and only inserts when absent; re-runs should not create duplicates. Optional `update=True` branch is implemented and used where refresh-on-rerun is desirable.
- Schema-drift tolerance exists as claimed: insert/update payloads are filtered against `information_schema.columns` before write.
- `seed_document_versions()` no-op is acceptable: comments match schema reality (FK points to different table) and audit documents API/page uses `grc_audit_documents`, not `grc_document_versions`.
- One expectation mismatch to note: some quoted totals are environment-dependent (pre-existing rows). For example, code creates 36 plan items by construction, not inherently 44 in a clean DB.

Security (serious, task-relevant):
- Predictable external portal tokens are seeded (`demo-token-001-do-not-use`, etc.) while `/external-portal/access/{token}` is unauthenticated token-based access. This creates guessable live links and potential unauthorized access to shared documents in non-isolated/demo-exposed environments.

Next actions (high-impact):
1) Replace seeded external `access_token` values with cryptographically random tokens (or seed as expired/revoked by default).
2) Add an optional post-seed verification summary that computes actual row counts per table to prevent mismatch between claimed and actual volumes.
3) Reduce hard dependency on user id `4` by resolving admin user dynamically (fallback create/lookup), improving portability and preventing FK failures outside the current demo tenant baseline.

arguments.content:
Full demo data seeding for every audit module

Rewrote backend/scripts/seed_demo.py from a thin ~50-row seed into a
comprehensive, deterministic, idempotent seed that populates every one of
the 17 audit modules with realistic data.

Volumes after a single run:
- Universe: 20 entities across 5 business units
- Plans: 6 (FY24/25/26 × annual+strategic) + 36 plan items
- Engagements: 20 across all statuses + 79 team members + 200 time entries
- Workpapers: 40 + 120 procedures + 20 sampling records
- Findings: 30 with full CCCE + 30 recommendations + 30 mgmt responses
- Issues: 25 + 90 action plan milestones + 15 follow-ups + 6 escalations
- Surveys: 8 + 48 responses
- Documents: 25
- Charter: 3 versions (v7/v8/v9) + 12 charter clauses
- CCM: 12 rules + 30 anomalies + 8 exceptions
- QAIP: 4 reviews + 20 assurance gaps
- Test Scripts: 12
- Skills/Capacity: 8 demo auditors created + ~48 skill rows + 36 monthly
  capacity allocations
- Audit Committee: 1 committee + 5 members + 3 meetings + 27 agenda items
  + 12 pre-reads + 9 resolutions
- Reports: 6 + 4 board packs
- External auditor portal: 4 sessions

Idempotency & determinism:
- All dates derived from BASE = datetime(2026,1,1); no NOW() in keys.
- Existence-check upserts on stable natural keys; verified two consecutive
  runs produce identical row counts in every seeded table.
- Optional update=True branch refreshes non-key fields on re-run without
  inserting duplicates (used for entities, committee, external sessions).
- Schema-aware column filtering tolerates schema drift.

Security:
- External auditor portal access tokens are now cryptographically random
  (secrets.token_urlsafe(32)) instead of guessable demo strings; 2 of the 4
  sessions are seeded as expired so unauthenticated portal access is not
  viable without re-issuance.

Notes / drift:
- seed_document_versions is intentionally a no-op: grc_document_versions
  FK targets grc_documents (a separate table), not grc_audit_documents
  used by the audit page.
- 8 demo auditor users (ialvarez, mokafor, ynakamura, phaque, dkovac,
  erivera, tnguyen, hjohnson) are created in public.grc_users and linked
  to tenant 2 via grc_tenant_users so skills/capacity FKs resolve.

def seed_document_versions(cur, audit_doc_ids):
    """Seed grc_documents (governance/policy docs) + grc_document_versions.

    The audit-side `grc_audit_documents` table is independent from
    `grc_documents`; the FK on `grc_document_versions.document_id` targets
    `grc_documents`. We seed a parallel set of governance documents (one per
    audit document title) and three versions each, so the document-versions
    relationship is populated and the table renders content if surfaced.
    """
    if not has_pub_table(cur, "grc_documents") or not audit_doc_ids:
        return
    # Build a small parallel set of governance docs (title-stable upsert key).
    gov_ids: list[int] = []
    for i, title in enumerate([t[0] for t in DOC_TITLES]):
        gov_ids.append(upsert(
            cur, "grc_documents",
            {"tenant_id": TENANT_ID, "title": title},
            {"doc_type": DOC_TITLES[i][1],
             "version": "v2.0",
             "status": "approved",
             "owner_id": 4,
             "approved_by": 4,
             "approved_at": d(-30),
             "published_by": 4,
             "published_at": d(-25),
             "review_cycle_months": 12,
             "next_review_date": d(335),
             "content": f"Governance document body for {title}.",
             "created_at": BASE},
        ))
    if not has_pub_table(cur, "grc_document_versions"):
        return
    for di, did in enumerate(gov_ids):
        for v, ver in enumerate(["v1.0", "v1.1", "v2.0"]):
            upsert(
                cur, "grc_document_versions",
                {"document_id": did, "version_number": ver},

def _snapshot_counts(conn) -> dict[str, int]:
    out: dict[str, int] = {}
    with conn.cursor() as cur:
        for table, where in VERIFY_TABLES:
            cur.execute(
                "SELECT 1 FROM information_schema.tables "
                "WHERE table_schema='public' AND table_name=%s",
                (table,),
            )
            if not cur.fetchone():
                continue
            cur.execute(f'SELECT COUNT(*) FROM "{table}" WHERE {where}')
            out[table] = cur.fetchone()[0]
    return out


def verify():
    """Run the seed twice and assert identical row counts (idempotency)."""
    print("[verify] === pass 1 ===")
    main()
    conn = psycopg2.connect(DSN)
    try:
        before = _snapshot_counts(conn)
    finally:
        conn.close()
    print("[verify] === pass 2 ===")
    main()
    conn = psycopg2.connect(DSN)
    try:
        after = _snapshot_counts(conn)
    finally:
        conn.close()

    print("\n[verify] table                                 before   after  delta")
    print("[verify] " + "-" * 64)
    failures = []
    for table in before:
        b = before[table]
        a = after[table]
        delta = a - b
        marker = " " if delta == 0 else "*"
        print(f"[verify] {marker} {table:42s} {b:7d} {a:7d} {delta:6d}")
        if delta != 0:
            failures.append((table, b, a))
    if failures:
        print(f"\n[verify] FAIL: {len(failures)} table(s) drifted across runs")
        for table, b, a in failures:
            print(f"[verify]   - {table}: {b} -> {a}")
        sys.exit(2)
    print(f"\n[verify] PASS: all {len(before)} tables stable across two runs")


if __name__ == "__main__":
    if len(sys.argv) > 1 and sys.argv[1] == "--verify":
        verify()
    else:
        main()

arguments.content:
Full demo data seeding for every audit module

Rewrote backend/scripts/seed_demo.py from a thin ~50-row seed into a
comprehensive, deterministic, idempotent seed that populates every audit
module with realistic data, plus a built-in --verify mode that runs the
seed twice and asserts identical row counts across all 41 seeded tables.

Volumes verified by --verify across 41 tables (sample):
- Universe: 20 entities across 5 business units
- Plans: 7 (multi-year) + 44 plan items
- Engagements: 20 + 79 team members + 200 time entries
- Workpapers: 40 + 120 procedures + 20 sampling records
- Findings: 30 + 30 recommendations + 30 mgmt responses
- Issues: 25 + 90 action plan milestones + 15 follow-ups + 6 escalations
- Surveys: 8 + 48 responses
- Audit Documents: 25; Governance Documents: 25 + 75 versions (3/doc)
- Charter: 7 versions + 19 clauses
- CCM: 12 rules + 30 anomalies + 8 exceptions
- QAIP: 6 reviews + 20 assurance gaps
- Test Scripts: 12; Skills: 59; Capacity: 38 monthly allocations
- Audit Committee: 1 + 5 members + 3 meetings + 27 agenda + 12 pre-reads
  + 9 resolutions
- Reports: 6; Board packs: 4; External auditor sessions: 4

Idempotency & determinism:
- All dates derived from BASE = datetime(2026,1,1); no NOW() in keys.
- Existence-check upserts on stable natural keys; `--verify` mode runs the
  seed twice and prints a delta table — currently PASS for all 41 tables.
- Optional update=True branch refreshes non-key fields without inserting
  duplicates (used for entities, committee, external sessions).
- Schema-aware column filtering tolerates schema drift.

Document versions (was previously a no-op):
- Now seeds a parallel set of `grc_documents` (governance docs, one per
  audit doc title) and 3 versions each in `grc_document_versions`, since
  the FK targets `grc_documents` rather than `grc_audit_documents`.

Security:
- External auditor portal access tokens are cryptographically random
  (secrets.token_urlsafe(32)) instead of guessable demo strings; 2 of 4
  sessions seeded as expired so unauthenticated portal access is not
  viable without re-issuance.

Notes / drift:
- 8 demo auditor users are created in public.grc_users and linked to
  tenant 2 via grc_tenant_users so skills/capacity FKs resolve.

Usage:
- `python3 backend/scripts/seed_demo.py`           — single seed pass
- `python3 backend/scripts/seed_demo.py --verify`  — seed twice + diff

def seed_audit_packages(cur, plan_ids):
    """Seed audit packages + access logs (used by external auditor portal /
    package sharing). Idempotent via stable name keys."""
    if not has_pub_table(cur, "grc_audit_packages"):
        return []
    pkg_specs = [
        ("FY26-Q1 SOX Package",        "draft",     -45),
        ("FY26-Q1 IT Controls Package","finalized", -30),
        ("FY26 AML Walkthroughs",      "finalized", -20),
        ("FY26 Treasury Quarterly",    "draft",     -10),
    ]
    pkg_ids = []
    for i, (name, status, off) in enumerate(pkg_specs):
        pkg_ids.append(upsert(
            cur, "grc_audit_packages",
            {"tenant_id": TENANT_ID, "name": name},
            {"description": f"Demo audit package: {name}.",
             "audit_period_start": d(off - 90),
             "audit_period_end":   d(off),
             "status": status,
             "created_by": 4,
             "finalized_at": d(off + 5) if status == "finalized" else None,
             "finalized_by": 4 if status == "finalized" else None,
             "is_legal_hold": False,
             "package_metadata": J({"plan_id": plan_ids[i % len(plan_ids)] if plan_ids else None,
                                   "items": ["walkthroughs", "samples", "memos"]}),
             "created_at": BASE},
        ))
    if not has_pub_table(cur, "grc_audit_package_access_logs"):
        return pkg_ids
    # Stable access-log entries: action+offset is part of the natural key so
    # re-runs do not duplicate rows.
    actions = ["view", "download", "share"]
    for pi, pid in enumerate(pkg_ids):
        for k in range(3):  # 3 log rows per package = 12 total
            upsert(
                cur, "grc_audit_package_access_logs",
                {"package_id": pid, "user_id": 4,
                 "action": actions[k], "accessed_at": d(-5 + pi + k)},
                {"ip_address": "10.0.0.1", "user_agent": "demo-seed/1.0"},
            )
    return pkg_ids

arguments.content:
Full demo data seeding for every audit module

Rewrote backend/scripts/seed_demo.py from a thin ~50-row seed into a
comprehensive, deterministic, idempotent seed that populates every audit
module and includes a built-in --verify mode that runs the seed twice and
asserts identical row counts across all 45 seeded tables.

Verified volumes (--verify PASS, 45 tables stable across two runs):
- Universe: 20 entities across 5 business units
- Plans: 7 + 44 plan items
- Engagements: 20 + 126 team members + 355 time entries
- Workpapers: 40 + 120 procedures + 20 sampling records
- Findings: 30 + 30 recommendations + 30 mgmt responses
- Issues: 25 + 90 action plan milestones + 15 follow-ups + 6 escalations
- Surveys: 8 + 48 responses
- Audit Documents: 25; Governance Documents: 25 + 75 versions (3/doc)
- Charter: 7 versions + 19 clauses + 24 engagement links + 12 plan links
- CCM: 12 rules + 30 anomalies + 8 exceptions
- QAIP: 6 reviews + 20 assurance gaps
- Test Scripts: 12; Auditors: 12 + 83 skills + 152 monthly capacity rows
  (12 users × 12 months + admin baseline)
- Audit Committee: 1 + 5 members + 3 meetings + 27 agenda + 12 pre-reads
  + 9 resolutions
- Reports: 6; Board packs: 4
- External auditor sessions: 4 + 4 audit packages + 12 access logs

Idempotency & determinism:
- All dates derived from BASE = datetime(2026,1,1); no NOW() in keys.
- Existence-check upserts on stable natural keys; `--verify` mode runs
  the seed twice, prints a delta table, and exits non-zero on drift.
- VERIFY_TABLES uses TENANT_ID (no hardcoded `2`) for portability.
- Optional update=True branch refreshes non-key fields on re-run.
- Schema-aware column filtering tolerates schema drift.

Charter clause links (newly added):
- grc_charter_clause_engagement_links: 2 engagements per clause (24)
- grc_charter_clause_plan_links: 1 plan per clause (12)

Audit packages + access logs (newly added):
- grc_audit_packages: 4 (FY26 SOX/IT/AML/Treasury), some finalized
- grc_audit_package_access_logs: 3 actions × 4 packages = 12

Document versions (was previously a no-op):
- Now seeds parallel `grc_documents` + 3 `grc_document_versions` per doc,
  since the FK targets `grc_documents` rather than `grc_audit_documents`.

Security:
- External auditor portal `access_token` values are cryptographically
  random (secrets.token_urlsafe(32)) generated only on first insert and
  intentionally NOT refreshed on re-run, so they remain both secure and
  deterministic. Half the sessions are seeded `expired` to limit
  unauthenticated portal exposure.

Notes:
- 12 demo auditor users created in public.grc_users and linked to
  tenant 2 via grc_tenant_users so skills/capacity FKs resolve.

Usage:
- `python3 backend/scripts/seed_demo.py`           — single seed pass
- `python3 backend/scripts/seed_demo.py --verify`  — seed twice + diff

Critical analysis:
- Idempotency strategy is materially improved and credible: stable natural-key `upsert` pattern is consistently used, `update=True` is available for controlled refreshes, and a two-pass `--verify` mode compares per-table counts to detect drift.

The screenshot redirected to login (session not shared with the tool), but the root cause is now confirmed by the token values:

- `--color-text-inverse: #FFFFFF` → the global rule `.text-white { color: #FFFFFF }` keeps white text white **everywhere**.
- `--color-surface: #FFFFFF` → `platform-ui` converts dark card backgrounds (`bg-slate-900/800/700`) to white.

The audit layout wraps everything in `bg-slate-950 text-slate-200` under the `platform-ui` parent, but **never applies the `.audit-light` class** — which is the one block that actually converts `text-white`/`text-slate-100/200/300` to dark. So white text designed for dark cards lands on white backgrounds → unreadable. Let me gauge the scope across audit pages.

You may depend on existing tasks that are PENDING or later — never on existing PROPOSED tasks. Tasks within the same batch may depend on each other freely.

# Fix Unreadable White-On-White Text

## What & Why
Text across the logged-in platform (audit, admin, ComplyChat) renders in light/white colors on white backgrounds, making it unreadable. The internal pages were originally built for a dark theme (`text-white`, `text-slate-100/200/300`, `bg-slate-900/800/700`), but the app now renders on a light surface. A theme-conversion system already exists in `globals.css` (the `.audit-light` block) that correctly converts dark-built markup to readable dark-on-light — including keeping white text on colored buttons. The bug is that this conversion is not actually applied to the pages: the audit layout wraps content in a dark slate background without the conversion class, and the global `platform-ui` overrides do not convert `text-white` / `text-slate-100/200/300`, so light text survives on the white surface.

## Done looks like
- Every logged-in page (all `/audit/*` pages, `/admin` and its sub-pages, `/complychat`) shows readable dark text on light backgrounds.
- Headings, body text, table cells, labels, and helper text are all legible — no white or near-white text on white/light surfaces.
- White text is preserved where it belongs: on solid colored buttons and badges (e.g. primary/blue, success/green, danger/red, gradient buttons).
- Inputs, selects, and textareas show dark text with visible borders and readable placeholders.
- The login page (already correct) is unchanged.

## Out of scope
- No redesign or restyling beyond fixing contrast/readability.
- No change to the color palette or brand tokens.
- No backend changes.

## Steps
1. **Apply the light-theme conversion to the audit module** — Make the audit section actually use the existing dark-to-light conversion wrapper instead of rendering on a raw dark background, so all audit pages convert to readable dark-on-light.
2. **Apply the same conversion to the remaining dark-built modules** — Ensure the admin pages (users, roles, audit-logs, organization) and the ComplyChat page receive the same conversion wrapper so their light text becomes readable too.
3. **Generalize / verify the conversion rules cover all cases** — Confirm the conversion handles `text-white`, `text-slate-100/200/300`, and `bg-slate-950` (the audit layout's own background), and that colored buttons/badges keep their white text. Extend the conversion block where a case is missing rather than editing individual pages.
4. **Verify across modules** — Log in to the demo tenant and visually confirm readability on representative pages in each module (audit dashboard plus a few sub-pages, admin, ComplyChat), checking tables, forms, cards, and buttons.

## Notes / constraints
- Prefer fixing at the theme/layout level (a few shared selectors and layout wrappers) over editing all 28+ page files individually. The conversion machinery already exists and is comprehensive — the primary defect is that it is not being applied.
- Key tokens: `--color-surface: #FFFFFF`, `--color-text: #1A1A1A`, `--color-text-inverse: #FFFFFF`. Note the global rule that forces `.text-white` to `--color-text-inverse` (white) is what keeps stray light text white on light surfaces.

- `frontend/src/app/globals.css:587-715`

- `frontend/src/app/(dashboard)/audit/layout.tsx`
- `frontend/src/app/(dashboard)/layout.tsx`

arguments.content:
Fix unreadable white-on-white text in audit module (Task #26)

Problem: All 28 /audit/* pages were built for a dark theme (text-white,
text-slate-100/200/300, bg-slate-800/900) but render on a light surface
under the platform-ui wrapper. The platform-ui overrides convert dark
card backgrounds to white but do NOT convert text-white or
text-slate-100/200/300, and the global rule `.text-white { color:
var(--color-text-inverse) }` (white) keeps that text white — producing
white text on white backgrounds.

Root cause: globals.css already contains a comprehensive dark->light
conversion block (`.audit-light`, lines 717-830) that correctly converts
all the dark text/bg classes AND preserves white text on colored
buttons/badges (lines 809-823). The audit layout simply never applied
the `.audit-light` class — it wrapped content in `bg-slate-950
text-slate-200` instead.

Fix: frontend/src/app/(dashboard)/audit/layout.tsx now wraps audit
content in `audit-light` and uses `!bg-[var(--color-subtle)]` for the
page canvas (the ! is needed because `.audit-light { background:#fff }`
would otherwise win on source order). This activates the existing
conversion across every audit page and their inline (non-portal) modals
at once — no per-page edits required.

Scope notes / deviations:
- Admin and ComplyChat were assessed and left unchanged: they are
  light-built (text-black/text-slate-600) and only use text-white on
  colored buttons, which already renders correctly. Applying audit-light
  there would have risked regressions (icon-badge transparency, canvas
  bg), so the fix was scoped to the genuinely-broken audit module.
- The only bg-slate-950 usage in audit was the layout itself, so no page
  needed the bg-slate-950 conversion gap closed.
- Visual verification via the screenshot tool was not possible because it
  cannot authenticate into the demo tenant; correctness was validated by
  CSS specificity/cascade reasoning and a clean /audit compile.
- The unrelated backend ResponseValidationError (is_primary None in
  tenants_router) was observed in logs but is out of scope for this task.
